channel.title: Direct Metal Sinter
channel.description: Recent content on Direct Metal Sinter

item.title: How DMLS Works: Laser Parameters, Build Strategy, and Process Physics
item.description: DMLS is often described as ’laser sintering metal powder’ but the physics is full melting, not sintering. Understanding the process at the parameter level is essential for design and quality.

item.title: About Direct Metal Sinter
item.description:
Direct Metal Laser Sintering — The Process That Changed Metal Manufacturing

Direct Metal Sinter is a dedicated resource for DMLS technology — the powder bed fusion process invented by EOS GmbH in 1987 that has become the backbone of metal additive manufacturing in aerospace, medical devices, and industrial tooling.

DMLS works by scanning a Yb:fiber laser (200–400W) across a bed of pre-alloyed powder (20–100 µm layer), fully melting each cross-section to build up a 99.5%-dense metal part without tooling. Despite the name, the process fully melts (not merely sinters) the powder.

item.title: Advertise on Direct Metal Sinter
item.description:
Advertise on Direct Metal Sinter

Direct Metal Sinter reaches engineers and procurement managers actively sourcing metal additive manufacturing services, materials, and equipment.

Advertising Packages

Sponsored Content $1,200/month Article feature
Network Package $3,500/month All PrintWire metal sites

Contact: maxdavisgroup@gmail.com

item.title: Services
item.description:
DMLS Production

DMLS (Direct Metal Laser Sintering) was invented by EOS GmbH in 1987 and remains the most qualified metal additive manufacturing process for aerospace and medical applications. A 200–400W Yb:fiber laser sinters pre-alloyed powder layer by layer (20–100 µm) to produce 99.5% dense parts. Six alloys certified across aerospace (AMS 4999, AS9100D) and medical (ASTM F136, ISO 13485) standards.

Process Engineering

Process engineering for DMLS covers laser power, scan speed, hatch spacing, and layer thickness optimization for energy density targeting (50–100 J/mm³). Support strategy balances residual stress management against post-processing cost. First-article inspection includes CMM dimensional verification, Archimedes density measurement, and mechanical testing per specification.
